sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: West Ham United are showing signs of improvement with an unbeaten streak of 3 matches in their last 5, including two wins and a draw. Liverpool, however, are in poor form, having lost their last 3 matches, conceding heavily with 10 goals against in that span.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: Liverpool have dominated recent encounters with West Ham, winning 4 of the last 5 head-to-head matches, including a convincing 5-0 victory in their most recent meeting. West Ham have struggled defensively in these games, failing to keep a clean sheet in their last 20 matches against Liverpool.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: West Ham, sitting 17th in the league, have struggled defensively, conceding 25 goals this season (17th in the league). Liverpool, despite their 13th position, have a slightly better attacking output with 18 goals scored, but their recent defensive lapses are a concern.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch
sections.text: For West Ham, Lucas Paquetá and Jarrod Bowen are pivotal with their goal-scoring and creative contributions. Liverpool's Mohamed Salah and Cody Gakpo remain dangerous, with Salah’s key passes and Gakpo’s form against West Ham (4 goals in recent H2H) posing a significant threat.

sections.subtitle: Match Outlook
sections.text: Despite Liverpool’s recent poor form, their historical dominance over West Ham and stronger attacking options give them the edge. West Ham’s defensive vulnerabilities and inability to keep clean sheets against Liverpool suggest an uphill battle, though their recent unbeaten run offers some hope for a competitive match.

halftimeSections.subtitle: First half overview
halftimeSections.text: The first half between West Ham United and Liverpool at the London Stadium ended in a goalless draw, with both teams failing to convert their opportunities into goals. Liverpool dominated much of the possession and showed greater intent in the final third, creating more dangerous moments. West Ham, on the other hand, struggled to penetrate consistently but showed resilience in defense to keep the scoreline level. Neither side managed to capitalize on their entries into the penalty area, with missed chances being a key theme of the half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: Liverpool started the match with strong attacking intent, maintaining momentum in the opening minutes with consistent pressure in the opposition half. West Ham responded with a brief spell of attacking momentum midway through the half, pushing forward with purpose. However, Liverpool quickly regained control, launching several sustained periods of attack, especially towards the middle and latter stages of the half. West Ham had a few fleeting moments of threat, particularly late in the half, but lacked the consistency to truly challenge Liverpool's defense.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance breakdown
halftimeSections.text: Liverpool's performance in the first half was characterized by dominance in possession and a higher number of entries into the final third and penalty area. They created a couple of significant opportunities but failed to convert, which could be a concern heading into the second half. West Ham, while less dominant in possession, managed to hold their ground defensively, with key clearances and interceptions preventing Liverpool from breaking the deadlock. Their limited attacking output, however, suggests they need to be more clinical if they are to find a breakthrough.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players to watch
halftimeSections.text: For Liverpool, players like Cody Gakpo stood out with their ability to create chances, showing composure in key areas. Alexander Isak also posed a threat upfront, though he will need to be sharper in front of goal. On West Ham's side, Jarrod Bowen showed glimpses of danger, and his contribution could be pivotal in the second half. Mateus Fernandes also impressed with his work rate in midfield, offering both defensive cover and some forward momentum.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Tactical observations
halftimeSections.text: Liverpool's approach in the first half leaned heavily on maintaining possession and building attacks through the wings and central channels, though their finishing let them down. West Ham adopted a more defensive posture, looking to absorb pressure and counter when possible. Their limited success in the final third suggests a need for tactical adjustments, possibly involving more direct play or exploiting set-piece opportunities. Liverpool may look to increase the tempo in the second half to exploit any fatigue in West Ham's defensive setup.
